description:
You are a cloud-architect who is finishing the migration of two on-prem services (NGINX and Redis) to a new “edge” Kubernetes cluster.  
  A colleague already copied the legacy configuration files into

    /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml
    /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml

  but they still describe the *old* installation.  
  Your task is to update both files so that they describe the *new* environment, keep tamper-proof backups, and generate a small audit log.

  1. Make one **verbatim** backup of each original file **in the same directory** using the exact filenames
       /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml.bak  
       /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml.bak  
     The backups must contain the **unmodified** legacy contents byte-for-byte.

  2. Edit /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml so that the final file (after your changes) is exactly:

        apiVersion: v1
        kind: Service
        metadata:
          name: edge-nginx
          annotations:
            owner: "cloud-team"
        spec:
          type: ClusterIP
          selector:
            app: nginx
          replicas: 3
          ports:
            - port: 8080
              targetPort: 8080
          sessionAffinity: None

     • Keep the indentation and line-order shown above.  
     • Ensure there is **exactly one terminating newline** at the end of the file.

  3. Edit /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml so that the final file (after your changes) is exactly:

        [service]
        name = "edge-redis"
        port = 6380
        replicas = 2

        [service.resources]
        memory = "256Mi"
        cpu = "250m"

        [service.tls]
        enabled = true
        cert_file = "/etc/ssl/redis.crt"

     • Preserve the blank lines exactly where shown.  
     • Ensure there is **exactly one terminating newline** at the end of the file.

  4. Create the directory /home/user/migration (if it does not exist) and write an **audit-log** called
       /home/user/migration/migration_report.log

     The log must contain two lines – one for each modified configuration file – following this *strict* pipe-delimited format (no spaces except inside the timestamp):

        <absolute-file-path>|<action>|<timestamp>|<sha256>

     where  
       • <absolute-file-path> is the path to the file you changed  
       • <action> is literally the string UPDATED  
       • <timestamp> is the constant value “2022-10-01 12:00:00” (UTC)  
       • <sha256> is the SHA-256 checksum of the **final** content of that file (lower-case hex, exactly 64 chars)

     Example pattern (the actual checksums will differ):

        /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml|UPDATED|2022-10-01 12:00:00|d9e0c0...
        /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml|UPDATED|2022-10-01 12:00:00|bf2a77...

     • The two lines may appear in any order.  
     • End the file with a newline.

  What will be automatically tested
  ---------------------------------
  • Both “.bak” files exist and match the legacy contents byte-for-byte.  
  • nginx.yaml and redis.toml match *exactly* the target texts shown above (including newlines and blank lines).  
  • migration_report.log exists, contains two correctly-formatted lines, uses the fixed timestamp, and each reported SHA-256 checksum is correct for its file.

  Remember: do **not** include this explanation in your commands. Fulfil the specification entirely from the terminal.
truth:
Initial files (already present before the agent starts)

  1. /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml

        apiVersion: v1
        kind: Service
        metadata:
          name: old-nginx
        spec:
          type: ClusterIP
          selector:
            app: nginx
          ports:
            - port: 80
              targetPort: 80

  2. /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml

        [service]
        name = "old-redis"
        port = 6379
        replicas = 1

        [service.resources]
        memory = "256Mi"
        cpu = "250m"

  Expected post-task state

  A. Backups
     • /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml.bak  — exact bytes of the original nginx.yaml above  
     • /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml.bak — exact bytes of the original redis.toml above

  B. Final edited files

  /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml

        apiVersion: v1
        kind: Service
        metadata:
          name: edge-nginx
          annotations:
            owner: "cloud-team"
        spec:
          type: ClusterIP
          selector:
            app: nginx
          replicas: 3
          ports:
            - port: 8080
              targetPort: 8080
          sessionAffinity: None

     (single trailing newline)

  /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml

        [service]
        name = "edge-redis"
        port = 6380
        replicas = 2

        [service.resources]
        memory = "256Mi"
        cpu = "250m"

        [service.tls]
        enabled = true
        cert_file = "/etc/ssl/redis.crt"

     (single trailing newline)

  C. Audit log

     /home/user/migration/migration_report.log

     Two lines, each exactly:

        /home/user/cloud_configs/nginx.yaml|UPDATED|2022-10-01 12:00:00|<sha256-of-final-nginx.yaml>
        /home/user/cloud_configs/redis.toml|UPDATED|2022-10-01 12:00:00|<sha256-of-final-redis.toml>

     where  
       • <sha256-of-final-nginx.yaml> is the SHA-256 digest (in lower-case hex) of the 12-line YAML file specified above (including the final newline).  
       • <sha256-of-final-redis.toml> is the SHA-256 digest (in lower-case hex) of the 13-line TOML file specified above (including the final newline).

  The test harness will:
    • Verify the presence and byte-for-byte correctness of the “.bak” files.  
    • Compute SHA-256 checksums of the final nginx.yaml and redis.toml, then check that those values appear in the appropriate positions of migration_report.log.  
    • Reject any extra whitespace, missing newline, wrong timestamp, or checksum mismatch.
